name: DataTree<T>
summary: Implements basic Grasshopper Data Tree functionality in an easy-to-use class. This class is used primarily in Scripting components. If you're using the Grasshopper SDK you should consider using the GH_Structure<T> class instead.

constructors.name: DataTree<T>()
constructors.summary: Default constructor, create an empty data tree.

constructors.name: DataTree<T>(DataTree<T>)
constructors.summary: Create a shallow duplicate of another data tree. This means it will create a new tree that contains the same items.

constructors.name: DataTree<T>(IEnumerable<T>, GH_Path)
constructors.signature: public DataTree(IEnumerable<T> data, GH_Path root = null)
constructors.summary: Create a tree with a single branch

params.name: data
params.doc: Data to append to the tree.

params.name: root
params.doc: Branch path of first branch. If Null, the default path is used.

constructors.name: DataTree<T>(DataTree<T>, DataTree<T>.DuplicateT)
constructors.summary: Create a true duplicate of another data tree.

params.name: copyDelegate
params.doc: Delegate function used to duplicate individual items.

methods.name: Add(T)
methods.summary: Add (append) a data item to the last branch in the tree. If no branches exist yet, a new one will be created with [path = {0}]

methods.name: Add(T, GH_Path)
methods.signature: public void Add(T data, GH_Path path)
methods.summary: Add (append) a data item to the specified branch in the tree. If the branch doesn't exist yet, it will be created.

methods.name: AddRange(IEnumerable<T>)
methods.summary: Add (append) a list of data items to the last branch in the tree. If no branch exists yet, a new one will be created.

methods.name: AddRange(IEnumerable<T>, GH_Path)
methods.signature: public void AddRange(IEnumerable<T> data, GH_Path path)
methods.summary: Add (append) a list of data items to the specified branch in the tree. If the branch doesn't exist yet, it will be created.

methods.name: AllData
methods.summary: Collects all data in the tree in a single list. Does not alter the topology of this tree.

returns.doc: A linear list containing all data items in all branches.

methods.name: Branch(Int32)
methods.summary: Gets the list of data which belongs to the branch path at the given index.

throws.when: Thrown if the index does not represent a valid path.

methods.name: Branch(Int32[])
methods.summary: Gets the list of data which belongs to a given Branch path.

methods.name: Branch(GH_Path)
methods.summary: Gets the list of data which belongs to a given Branch path.

methods.name: Clear
methods.summary: Clears the entire tree.

methods.name: ClearData
methods.summary: Removes all data from all branches without affecting the tree topology.

methods.name: EnsurePath(Int32[])
methods.summary: Create a new branch with the specified path if it doesn't already exists.

params.name: path
params.doc: Path of the branch to ensure.

returns.doc: The list that belongs to the specified path.

methods.name: EnsurePath(GH_Path)
methods.summary: Create a new branch with the specified path if it doesn't already exists.

params.name: path
params.doc: Path of the branch to ensure.

returns.doc: The list that belongs to the specified path.

methods.name: Flatten
methods.summary: Flattens the entire tree into a single path.

params.name: flatten_path
params.doc: The path of the flattened data. If null, the path will be {0}

methods.name: Graft(Boolean)
methods.summary: Graft all paths in this tree. "Grafting" means appending a new branch for every item in an existing branch.

params.name: skip_nulls
params.doc: If true, null items in the grafted path will not be included in the new tree.

methods.name: Graft(GH_Path, Boolean)
methods.signature: public void Graft(GH_Path path, bool skip_nulls = false)
methods.summary: Graft a single path in the tree. "Grafting" means appending a new branch for every item in an existing branch.

params.name: skip_nulls
params.doc: If true, null items in the grafted path will not be included in the new tree.

methods.name: Insert
methods.signature: public void Insert(T data, GH_Path path, int index)
methods.summary: Insert a data item to the specified branch in the tree. If the branch doesn't exist yet, it will be created.

methods.name: ItemExists
methods.signature: public bool ItemExists(GH_Path path, int index)
methods.summary: Test if the specified path + item index are defined inside the tree.

returns.doc: True if the specified path exists and contains the specified index.

methods.name: MergeTree
methods.summary: Merges two trees together. Data inside similar branches will be merged into single lists and unique paths will be appended. The other tree will not be altered, so beware that data is now shared among both trees.

throws.when: Thrown if other is a null reference.

methods.name: Path
methods.summary: Gets the data path at the specified index.

throws.when: Thrown if the index does not represent a valid path.

methods.name: PathExists(Int32[])
methods.summary: Test if the specified path is already defined inside the tree.

params.name: path
params.doc: The branch path to search for.

returns.doc: True if the path already exists, false if it doesn't.

methods.name: PathExists(GH_Path)
methods.summary: Test if the specified path is already defined inside the tree.

params.name: path
params.doc: The branch path to search for

returns.doc: True if the path already exists, false if it doesn't.

methods.name: RemovePath(Int32[])
methods.summary: Removes a path and all associated data from the structure. If the path doesn't exist, nothing will happen.

methods.name: RemovePath(GH_Path)
methods.summary: Removes a path and all associated data from the structure. If the path doesn't exist, nothing will happen.

methods.name: RenumberPaths()
methods.summary: Renumber all paths in this data tree, using a single incrementing path index.

methods.name: RenumberPaths(String)
methods.summary: Renumber all paths in this data tree, using a single incrementing path index.

params.name: mask
params.doc: String formatting mask for paths. Use semi-colons for path index separators and "{i}" as a path index placeholder. Examples: "{i}" -> {i}"0;{i}" -> {0;i}"{i};0;0" -> {i;0;0}

methods.name: SimplifyPaths
methods.summary: Simplify the branches in this tree by removing all identical path entries. The length of the shortest path will be indicative of the similarity search depth. If this tree only contains a single branch, the branch wil be simplified to its last index.

methods.name: ToString
methods.summary: Creates a brief description of the tree.

returns.doc: A String representing the current state of the data tree.

methods.name: TrimExcess
methods.summary: Trims the excess allocated memory in all branches

properties.name: BranchCount
properties.summary: Gets the number of branches defined in this tree.

properties.name: Branches
properties.summary: Gets a list of all the data-arrays in this tree

properties.name: DataCount
properties.summary: Gets the total number of data items (including nulls) stored in all branches.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Gets or set the data item at the specified path and index.

properties.name: Paths
properties.summary: Gets a list of all the branch paths in this tree.

properties.name: TopologyDescription
properties.summary: Gets a description of the topology of the tree. Useful for debugging purposes.
